title: Mexican Duck Stir-Fry
description: A spicy and flavorful stir-fry dish featuring the rich taste of duck, inspired by traditional Mexican cuisine.
instructions: 1. Season the duck breast with salt, pepper, and Mexican chili powder.
2. Heat olive oil in a large skillet over medium-high heat. Sear the duck breast for 2-3 minutes per side, or until browned. Remove from the skillet and set aside.
3. Add more oil to the skillet if necessary, then sauté the onion, bell pepper, and garlic until tender.
4. Stir in cumin, stir-fry sauce, and salsa. Bring to a simmer.
5. Return the duck breast to the skillet and spoon some of the sauce over the top.
6. Reduce heat to low and cook for an additional 5-7 minutes, or until the duck is cooked through.
7. Garnish with fresh cilantro and serve.
8. Slice the duck breast into thin strips before serving.
